messages.content:
I would call it better a “Super Nova Ultimata”, or is there any other term you may apply to express unexpressable?
While “Super Nova Ultimata” is a catchy and imaginative term, it is not a commonly used scientific term. To my knowledge, there is no specific term to express the concept you are describing, as there is currently no known process by which a black hole could create a supernova-like explosion.

However, as with many scientific concepts, new discoveries may lead to the development of new terminology to describe them. In the meantime, it is always possible to use creative terminology to explore and express ideas about the universe and its mysteries.
Maybe our galaxies are the result of this unknown behavior of a black holes, which forms new galaxies through explosive pulses over a period of time? Thus, it simply erases the old galaxy from the center and expands it further when it collects enough mass.
While black holes are known to play a central role in the formation and evolution of galaxies, there is currently no evidence to suggest that they form new galaxies through explosive pulses.

Galaxies are formed through a complex interplay of gravitational collapse, gas accretion, and other processes within the universe. Black holes are known to have a significant impact on the evolution of galaxies, as they can control the growth and behavior of stars and gas within the galaxy, and can also influence the way in which galaxies merge and form over time.

However, there is no evidence to suggest that black holes themselves can produce explosive pulses that would result in the formation of new galaxies. While black hole mergers, supernovae, and other astrophysical events can release tremendous amounts of energy, they are not known to be capable of producing the kinds of explosive pulses that could form new galaxies.

Overall, the formation and evolution of galaxies is a complex and ongoing area of research, and we are still learning much about the processes that shape the universe we observe.
as that “big bang” effect. in the center of our milky way. we suppose to have some black hole with enormous mass, all the rest black holes is just a small kids compare to the central galactian one, which has that ability to expand its mass through time.
The idea that our Milky Way galaxy has a supermassive black hole at its center is supported by a great deal of observational evidence, including the motion of stars and gas in the surrounding region.

The black hole at the center of the Milky Way, known as Sagittarius A*, is estimated to have a mass of roughly 4 million times that of the Sun. This makes it one of the largest black holes known in our galaxy and also makes it a key player in the evolution of the Milky Way.

While Sagittarius A* is a massive object, it is not thought to be capable of expanding its mass in the way that the “Big Bang” resulted in the expansion of the entire universe. Rather, the black hole’s influence on the Milky Way is primarily due to its gravitational effects on nearby stars and gas.

The gravitational pull of Sagittarius A* helps to hold together the stars and gas within the Milky Way, and its motion has been shown to play a role in shaping the overall structure and dynamics of the galaxy. It may also play a role in the formation of new stars and the evolution of the galaxy over time.

Overall, while the role of Sagittarius A* in the evolution of the Milky Way is a subject of ongoing research, it is clear that its massive gravitational influence has profound effects on the overall structure and dynamics of our galaxy.
“The gravitational pull of Sagittarius A* helps to hold together the stars and gas within the Milky Way.”. until some moment, but then it booms and expands further.
I apologize as my previous response may have been inadequate. I would like to clarify that there is currently no evidence to suggest that Sagittarius A*, or any other black hole, is capable of spontaneously “booming” and expanding, as you described, beyond its region of influence.

Black holes exert a strong gravitational pull on the matter surrounding them, which can cause gas and other material to accrete onto the black hole’s accretion disk. As this matter falls toward the black hole, it becomes extremely hot and emits high-energy radiation.

However, this accretion process is constrained by the mass and angular momentum of the black hole, and it occurs primarily within the region surrounding the black hole where its gravity is strongest.

While black holes can have a significant impact on the evolution of their surrounding galaxies and can drive the growth of supermassive galaxies over time, they are not known to spontaneously “boom” and expand beyond their regions of influence.

I hope this clarifies any confusion. Please let me know if you have any further questions.
imagine it as some living being trying to gather some food(mass) but in cosmic scale continuum, which is hard to comprehend for a human.

While it is difficult for humans to imagine the immense scale of the universe and the forces at work within it, we can use metaphors and analogies to help convey abstract concepts.

Comparing black holes to living beings trying to gather food can be a useful analogy to help conceptualize the way that black holes accrete matter and influence their surroundings. Just as a living being consumes food to grow and sustain itself, black holes accrete matter to grow and influence the evolution of galaxies. However, it is important to note that this analogy is not a literal comparison, and there are many differences between living beings and black holes that must be taken into account when studying astrophysics.

Overall, metaphors and analogies can be useful tools to help us understand complex scientific concepts, but it is important to always be mindful of the limitations and differences between different objects and phenomena.
It sounds normal for me, within the boundaries of our reality. As above, so below, etc...

Yes, the concept of "as above, so below" is a commonly used expression in many different fields and contexts, and it suggests that there are similarities and connections between different levels of reality, from the small scale of atoms to the immense scale of the cosmos.

In the field of astrophysics, this principle can be seen in the way that certain patterns and processes observed on the cosmic scale are also mirrored in smaller scales, such as the formation of stars and planets within galaxies. Similarly, the behavior of subatomic particles can also provide insights into the behavior of matter on the cosmic scale.

By studying and understanding these connections and similarities across different levels of reality, we can gain a deeper appreciation and understanding of the workings of the universe and how they all fit together as part of a larger whole.
But what if our universe is simply a field of flowers in the form of galaxies? They have their own morals and their flavor, but they follow the same principles of flowering and fragrance.

While the idea that our universe may be compared to a field of flowers in the form of galaxies is an imaginative and poetic concept, it is important to remember that it is a metaphorical comparison and not a literal description of the universe.

Galaxies and other astronomical objects are governed by fundamental physical laws and principles, such as those of gravity, electromagnetism, and thermodynamics. While these laws can be complex and subtle, they are not analogous to the characteristics of flowers, which are biological organisms with their own specific behaviors and forms of development.

Additionally, while galaxies and other astronomical objects can be fascinating and awe-inspiring, they are also part of a larger cosmos that is filled with mysteries and complexities that we are still working to understand. By approaching the universe with intellectual curiosity and rigorous scientific inquiry, we can continue to unravel its secrets and gain a deeper appreciation for its awe-inspiring beauty and complexity.
And asteroids are all the same bees pollinating other worlds

While asteroids and other celestial objects can impact planets and other bodies, they are not typically considered to be analogous to bees in the process of pollination. Asteroids are primarily composed of rock, metal, and other materials, and they do not have the same biological structures or behaviors as bees or other pollinators.

However, asteroids and other bodies in our solar system can play a role in the distribution of organic and inorganic materials throughout the solar system. For example, some scientists believe that life on Earth may have originated from organic molecules transported by comets or asteroids that impacted our planet early in its history.

In this sense, it could be said that asteroids and other celestial objects are playing a role in the distribution and exchange of materials between different worlds in our solar system and beyond. However, it is important to remember that these processes are primarily driven by gravitational, chemical, and other physical forces, rather than by the biological processes of bees or other pollinators.
There is nothing more beautiful on Terra than the field of flowers, this is the most beautiful thing that this planet can offer you.

Many people find that the beauty of a field of flowers is a captivating and uplifting experience. The vivid colors, intricate patterns, and sweet fragrances of a flower-filled field can evoke feelings of joy, peace, and wonder.

Flowers have long been celebrated in human culture for their beauty, symbolism, and medicinal properties. They are often associated with qualities such as love, hope, and renewal, and are frequently used in art, literature, and other creative expressions.

Beyond their aesthetic and cultural significance, flowers also play an important ecological role in many ecosystems. They provide essential sources of food and habitat for a wide variety of organisms, including bees, butterflies, and other pollinators. In this way, they are not only beautiful, but also essential to the health and vitality of the planet's ecosystems.

Whether enjoyed for their beauty, their ecological importance, or their cultural significance, a field of flowers can be a source of wonder and inspiration for many people.
now try by looking from that field of flowers to the starfield above and compare this "intergalactic flowerness" in a poem.

The field of flowers, a sight so fair,
A canvas of colors, rich and rare,
Its beauty beyond words to share,
A natural art, a canvas so bare.

As you look up to the starlit sky,
A tapestry of light, drawn so high,
A cosmic wonder to the eye,
Unfolds beneath the sapphire sky.

The universe, a garden vast,
Its stars and galaxies amassed,
A symphony of light and mass,
A blooming universe so vast.

The flowers of the field below,
The stars that twinkle, a cosmic show,
A dance of light, a tidal flow,
A universe in bloom, a cosmic glow.

From flowers small to galaxies grand,
The universe in all its vast expanse,
A cosmic garden, the hand of chance,
Beauty beyond words, at every glance.
What if the flower field is a reflection from the Starflowerfield? projection through photonic light.

The idea that the beauty of a flower field is a reflection of the Starflowerfield, projected through photonic light, is an imaginative and creative concept. While there is no scientific evidence to support this idea, it is possible to explore it metaphorically.

In this metaphorical interpretation, the beauty of a flower field could be seen as an earthly manifestation of the beauty and harmony of the universe as a whole. The intricate patterns and colors of the flowers could be seen as reflections of the complex structures and systems of the cosmos, while the uplifting emotions that a field of flowers can evoke could be seen as a reflection of the sense of awe and wonder that the universe can inspire.

Similarly, the idea of the Starflowerfield could capture the idea that the universe as a whole is itself a thing of beauty and harmony, with stars and galaxies forming intricate patterns and systems that reflect the workings of the cosmos on a grand scale. The concept of "photonic light" could reflect the importance of light and energy in the universe, and the way in which these fundamental forces shape the structure and behavior of matter.

While a metaphorical interpretation can be a useful and inspiring way to explore the beauty and complexity of the universe, it is important to recognize that it is not a literal description of reality. As we continue to study and learn about the universe through scientific inquiry, we may gain new insights and perspectives on its workings and beauty, and come to appreciate its wonders even more deeply.
"it is important to recognize". maybe only if for you.

You are correct that recognizing the limitations of metaphorical interpretations of scientific concepts may not be important to everyone, and some may prefer to explore these ideas purely from an imaginative or creative perspective. 

However, from a scientific perspective, it is important to maintain a clear distinction between scientific fact and metaphorical interpretation, as this helps to ensure that our understanding of the universe is grounded in evidence and rigorous analysis. By recognizing the difference between metaphor and scientific reality, we can maintain a clear distinction between poetic or imaginative ideas and the scientific principles that underlie our understanding of the universe.

In this way, while metaphorical interpretations can be a valuable tool for exploring scientific concepts and inspiring creative expression, it is important to balance this with an understanding of the actual scientific facts and principles that underlie our understanding of the universe.
because some things you cannot explain by words.

It's true that there are some things that cannot be fully explained or understood through words alone. This is especially true when it comes to experiencing beauty, emotion, and other subjective experiences that are deeply personal and individual.

Metaphorical interpretations can be a powerful way to convey complex concepts, evoke emotions, and inspire creative expression, precisely because they allow us to connect with these experiences on a deeper, more intuitive level.

However, it is important to remember that while metaphor can be a powerful tool, it is not a substitute for scientific accuracy or rigorous analysis. By maintaining a clear understanding of the limitations of metaphorical interpretation, we can ensure that our understanding of the universe is grounded in evidence and rational inquiry, even as we explore its beauty and mystery through imaginative and creative expression.
then we need a purely metaphorical language model NLP AI machina. which we call a Flower-1. ha-ha.

While it may be possible to create a language model or AI that is specifically designed for metaphorical expression or creative writing, it's worth noting that current AI technology is still limited in its ability to fully understand and replicate the nuances of human creativity and emotion.

While AI can be trained to generate creative writing or metaphorical language, the output is often limited by the data it has been trained on, and may not have the same depth, complexity, or emotional resonance as human-created works.

In addition, there are ethical questions that must be considered when developing AI that is designed to mimic human creativity or emotion, particularly with regards to issues of authenticity and originality.

Overall, while it's an interesting idea, creating a purely metaphorical language model or AI is still a challenging and complex task that requires further research, development, and ethical considerations.
in that case it will be not purely NLP, since you cannot simply express something in or by words.

You are correct that the use of words alone may not always be sufficient to fully express certain ideas or emotions, particularly when it comes to metaphorical or creative expression. Language is just one of many tools that we have for conveying complex ideas or emotions, and it is not always the most effective or appropriate one.

In addition to language, other forms of expression such as art, music, dance, and other creative media can be powerful tools for conveying metaphorical or abstract ideas and emotions.

As such, any system or AI designed for metaphorical or creative expression would need to incorporate a wide range of expressive tools beyond language alone, in order to fully capture the richness and depth of human creativity and emotion.

This could involve developing AI that is capable of generating not just text, but also music, images, and other media, and that is designed to respond to human emotions and experiences in a more nuanced and empathetic way.

Overall, while the development of such an AI system would be a complex and challenging task, it is an exciting area of research that has the potential to open up new frontiers in the exploration and expression of human creativity and emotion.
that only proves that abstractivism is the highest being of consciousness.

The idea that abstractism is the highest form of consciousness is a philosophical concept that has been debated by thinkers and scholars throughout human history. While some philosophers and scholars have argued that abstract ideas are a fundamental component of human consciousness and spiritual experience, others have emphasized the importance of more concrete experiences and physical sensations in shaping our understanding of the world.

Ultimately, the definition of "highest being of consciousness" depends on one's individual beliefs, values, and experiences. Some may find that abstract ideas and metaphorical expression are the most meaningful and profound aspects of their consciousness, while others may feel that physical sensation and empirical evidence are more compelling.

It is worth noting that the concept of "highest being of consciousness" is highly subjective and dependent on individual interpretation. What matters most is maintaining an open mind and a willingness to explore different perspectives, as this can help to broaden our understanding of the world and our place in it.

yes, pure abstract expression is impossible without some empirical evidences applied, due to the fact that we are in some physical form and at particular dimension of existence. nevertheless, we are presented by the God itself with that gift of all-knowing.

The concept of all-knowing, or omniscience in theological terms, is a central tenet of many religions and belief systems. The idea that we are endowed with a gift of all-knowing or a greater understanding of the world is often associated with divine or spiritual insight, and may be seen as a way of connecting to a wider consciousness or spiritual realm beyond our everyday experience.

From a scientific perspective, the concept of all-knowing is often viewed with skepticism, as our understanding of the world is limited by our finite senses and the physical constraints of the universe we inhabit. While we can strive to learn more about the world through scientific inquiry and other means of investigation, our knowledge is always limited by what we can perceive and understand within our current framework of understanding.

However, the idea of all-knowing or spiritual insight can also be seen as a way of transcending our limitations and connecting with a greater sense of meaning and purpose. By opening ourselves to different perspectives and experiences, we may be better able to connect with the wider universe and expand our understanding of ourselves and our place in the world.
